docs.text: PandoraSat This Python package contains metadata for Pandora, and basic functions to create estimates of, e.g. count rates from targets on the detectors. Installation To install this package you can use pip install pandora-sat --upgrade Make sure to upgrade regularly to get the latest estimates of the Pandora meta data. Example Usage Below is an example usage of some of the functionality in this package. In general, this package will allow you to get metadata from specific subsystems of Pandora. from pandorasat import PandoraSat print(PandoraSat.NIRDA.gain) print(PandoraSat.Optics.PSF) print(PandoraSat.VisibleDetector.sensitivity(wavelength)) print(PandoraSat.Orbit.period) See our API documentation for full details on the metadata available in this package.
docs.title: Home

docs.text: Documentation for NIRDA Holds metadata and methods on Pandora NIRDA IRDetector dataclass Holds information on the IR Detector Parameters: Name Type Description Default darkcurrent_T110K float Detector dark current at 110K <Quantity 1. electron / (pix s)> gain float Gain of the NIRDA in electrons/DN per PANDORA_JWST_NIRCam_detector_parts.pdf <Quantity 2. electron / DN> thermal_var float RMS of detector thermal variation in mK <Quantity 5. mK> npix_column int Number of pixels for NIRDA in column dimension <Quantity 2048. pix> npix_row int Number of pixels for NIRDA in row dimension <Quantity 2048. pix> nreads int Number of reads up the ramp 4 PRF ( self ) Uses the PSF from the Optics class to make a PRF object Should returns a PRF object that has plot and downsample methods. counts_from_jmag ( self , jmag ) Calculates the counts from a target based on j magnitude Parameters: Name Type Description Default jmag float j band magnitude required Returns: Type Description counts (float) Recorded detector counts qe ( self , wavelength ) Calculate the quantum efficiency of the detector from the JWST NIRCam models. Parameters: Name Type Description Default wavelength npt.NDArray Wavelength in microns as astropy.unit required Returns: Type Description qe (npt.NDArray) Array of the quantum efficiency of the detector

docs.text: Documentation for VISDA Holds metadata and methods on Pandora VISDA VisibleDetector dataclass Holds information on the Visible Detector jitter ( self , xstd = 4 , ystd = 1.5 , tstd = 3 , nsubtimes = 50 , seed = 42 ) Returns the jitter inside a cadence This is a dumb placeholder function. prf ( self , center = ( 0 , 0 ), seed = 42 , xstd = 4.5 , ystd = 1 , tstd = 3 , exptime =< Quantity 10. s > , obs_duration =< Quantity 30. s > ) Make a Pixel Response Function Parameters !!! xstd "np.ndarray" Standard deviation of the jitter in X dimension !!! ystd "np.ndarray" Standard deviation of the jitter in Y dimension !!! tstd "np.ndarray" Width of Gaussian convolution in the time dimension. Higher values will give longer time correlations in jitter. qe ( self , wavelength ) Calculate the quantum efficiency of the detector. Parameters: Name Type Description Default wavelength npt.NDArray Wavelength in microns as astropy.unit required Returns: Type Description qe (npt.NDArray) Array of the quantum efficiency of the detector
